rationale: In the citing paper, Chandon and Wansink (2002) is cited only once in an introductory parenthetical list of prior work on purchase-quantity decisions. The article does not engage that paper's convenience-salience framework, stockpiling mechanisms, or specific findings when developing its quantity-matching theory or experiments. Because the reference functions as general background positioning rather than a distinct conceptual or methodological input, this is a passing mention.
